1.texto_original: Depois destas coisas e destes actos de fidelidade, veio Senaquerib, rei da Assíria e, entrando em Judá, acampou-se contra as cidades fortes, a fim de apoderar-se delas.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 32:1

"Depois destas coisas e destes actos de fidelidade, veio Senaquerib, rei da Assíria e, entrando em Judá, acampou-se contra as cidades fortes, a fim de apoderar-se delas."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Crônicas 32:1

Philological Analysis

"Depois destas coisas e destes actos de fidelidade, veio Senaquerib, rei da Assíria e, entrando em Judá, acampou-se contra as cidades fortes, a fim de apoderar-se delas."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

14.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 32:14

"Qual é, de todos os deuses daquelas nações que meus pais destruíram, o que pôde livrar o seu povo da minha mão, para que o vosso Deus vos possa livrar da minha mão?"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Crônicas 32:14

Philological Analysis

"Qual é, de todos os deuses daquelas nações que meus pais destruíram, o que pôde livrar o seu povo da minha mão, para que o vosso Deus vos possa livrar da minha mão?"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

27.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 32:27

"E teve Ezequias riquezas e honra em grande abundância; proveu-se de tesourarias para prata, ouro, pedras preciosas, especiarias, escudos, e toda espécie de objectos desejáveis;"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
27.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Crônicas 32:27

Philological Analysis

"E teve Ezequias riquezas e honra em grande abundância; proveu-se de tesourarias para prata, ouro, pedras preciosas, especiarias, escudos, e toda espécie de objectos desejáveis;"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
27.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"E teve Ezequias riquezas e honra em grande abundância; proveu-se de tesourarias para prata, ouro, pedras preciosas, especiarias, escudos, e toda espécie de objectos desejáveis;"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

31.texto_original: Contudo, no negócio dos embaixadores dos príncipes de Babilónia, que lhe foram enviados a perguntarem acerca do prodígio que fora feito na sua terra, Deus o desamparou para experimentá-lo, e para saber tudo o que havia no seu coração.

33.texto_original: E Ezequias dormiu com seus pais, e o sepultaram no mais alto dos sepulcros dos filhos de David; e todo o Judá e os habitantes de Jerusalém lhe renderam honras na sua morte. E Manassés, seu filho, reinou em seu lugar.

Devotional Study of 2 Crônicas 32:33

"E Ezequias dormiu com seus pais, e o sepultaram no mais alto dos sepulcros dos filhos de David; e todo o Judá e os habitantes de Jerusalém lhe renderam honras na sua morte. E Manassés, seu filho, reinou em seu lugar."
